GEORGE R.


N E£ORGE, by the Grace of Gop, King of Great-B;;.
' tain, France, and Ireland, Defender of the Faith, Ec.
I To all to whom theſe Preſents ſhall come, Greeting,
Whereas Our oy and Well-beloved BERNARD LinTor
of our City of London, Bookſeller, has humbly repreſented unto
Us that he is now printing a Tranſlation of the IL IAD of Ho-
MER, from the Greek, in Six Volumes in Folio, by Ar Ex-
ANDER Por Gent. with large Notes upon each Book: And
whereas the ſaid BERNARD LinToT has informed Us that he
Has been at a great Expence in carrying on the ſaid Work: and
that the ſole Right and Title of the Copy of the ſaid Work is
veſted in the ſaid BERNARD LinToT: He has therefore
* Humbly beſought Us to grant him Our Royal Privilege and
Licence for the ſole Printing and Publiſhing thereof for the Term
of fourteen Years. Wx being graciouſly pleaſed to encourage fo
uſeful a Work, are pleaſed to condeſcend to his Requeſt ;z and do |
therefore hereby give and grant unto the ſaid BERNARD Lin-
or Our-Royal Licence and Privilege for the ſole printing and
publiſhing the faid Six Volumes of the IL IAD of Homes,
tranſlated by the ſaid AL EKXANDER Por E, for and during the
Term of fourteen Years, te be computed from the Day of the
Date hereof, ſtrictly charging and prohibiting all Our Subjects
within Our Kingdoms and Dominions to reprint or abridge the
ſame, either in the like or any other Volume or Volumes what-
ſoever; or to import, buy, vend, utter or diſtribute any Copies
of the ſame, or any part thereof reprinted beyond the Seas, with-
5n the ſaid Term of fourteen Years, without the Conſent and
Approbation of the ſaid BERNARD LIN Tor, his Heirs, Exe-
cutors and Aſſigns, by Writing under his or their Hands and
Seals firſt had and obtained, as they and every of them offending
"Herein will anſwer the contrary at their Perils, and ſuch other
Penalties as by the Laws and Statutes of this Our Realm may
be inflicted: W hereof the Maſter, Wardens and Company of Sta-
tioners of Our City of London, Commiſſioners 'and other Officers
of Our Cuſtoms, and all other Our Officers and Miniſters whom
it may concern, are to take Notice, that due Obedience be given
to Our Pleaſure herein ſignified, Giy at Our Court at St.
_ the fixth Day of May, 18 firſt V ear of Our
| 1 *


== =


By His Majeſty's Command, F=xq


Frs TAN HOPE. p.


We
Fa


GEORGE R.


N E£ORGE, by the Grace of Gop, King of Great-B;;.
' tain, France, and Ireland, Defender of the Faith, Ec.
I To all to whom theſe Preſents ſhall come, Greeting,
Whereas Our oy and Well-beloved BERNARD LinTor
of our City of London, Bookſeller, has humbly repreſented unto
Us that he is now printing a Tranſlation of the IL IAD of Ho-
MER, from the Greek, in Six Volumes in Folio, by Ar Ex-
ANDER Por Gent. with large Notes upon each Book: And
whereas the ſaid BERNARD LinToT has informed Us that he
Has been at a great Expence in carrying on the ſaid Work: and
that the ſole Right and Title of the Copy of the ſaid Work is
veſted in the ſaid BERNARD LinToT: He has therefore
* Humbly beſought Us to grant him Our Royal Privilege and
Licence for the ſole Printing and Publiſhing thereof for the Term
of fourteen Years. Wx being graciouſly pleaſed to encourage fo
uſeful a Work, are pleaſed to condeſcend to his Requeſt ;z and do |
therefore hereby give and grant unto the ſaid BERNARD Lin-
or Our-Royal Licence and Privilege for the ſole printing and
publiſhing the faid Six Volumes of the IL IAD of Homes,
tranſlated by the ſaid AL EKXANDER Por E, for and during the
Term of fourteen Years, te be computed from the Day of the
Date hereof, ſtrictly charging and prohibiting all Our Subjects
within Our Kingdoms and Dominions to reprint or abridge the
ſame, either in the like or any other Volume or Volumes what-
ſoever; or to import, buy, vend, utter or diſtribute any Copies
of the ſame, or any part thereof reprinted beyond the Seas, with-
5n the ſaid Term of fourteen Years, without the Conſent and
Approbation of the ſaid BERNARD LIN Tor, his Heirs, Exe-
cutors and Aſſigns, by Writing under his or their Hands and
Seals firſt had and obtained, as they and every of them offending
"Herein will anſwer the contrary at their Perils, and ſuch other
Penalties as by the Laws and Statutes of this Our Realm may
be inflicted: W hereof the Maſter, Wardens and Company of Sta-
tioners of Our City of London, Commiſſioners 'and other Officers
of Our Cuſtoms, and all other Our Officers and Miniſters whom
it may concern, are to take Notice, that due Obedience be given
to Our Pleaſure herein ſignified, Giy at Our Court at St.
_ the fixth Day of May, 18 firſt V ear of Our

Tranſlated by Mr. POPE.


Men moveat cimex Pantilius ? Aut crucier, quad


Vellicat abſentem Demetrius? Aut quod ineptus


Fannius Hermogenis lædat conviva Tigelli ?
Plotius, & Varius, Mæcenat, Virgiliuſque,
Valgius, & probet hac Ofavius Optimus | wes HI OR.


The FourRTH EpITION.


LOND ON:


Printed for BERNARD LINTOT, near
Temple-Bar. ;

REZA
The ARGUMENT.


The fourth battel continued, in which
Neptune aſſiſts the Greeks : The ad
of Idomeneus. |


F Eptune, concern'd for the loſi of the Grecians, au
Seeing the fortification forc d by Hector, (who hal
enter d the gate near the ftaticn of the Ajaxes) aſſumes th
Shape of Calchas, and inſpires thoſe heroes to oppoſe him:
Then in the form of one of the generals, encourages ti
other Greeks who had retir'd to their veſſels. The Ajaxe
form their troops in a cloſe Phalanx, and put a ſtop ti
Hector and the Trojans. Several deeds of valour ar


*


perform d; Meriones ing his ſpear in the encounter, ri.


pairs ie ſeek another at the tent of Idomeneus: This cl.
cafions a converſation between thoſe two warriours, wh
return ether to the battel. Idomeneus fignalizes his cou-
rage above the reft ; he kills Othryoneus, Aſius, and Al.
cathous : Deiphobus and Eneas march againſt him, ani
at length Idomeneus retires. Menelaus wounds Helenus
end kills Piſander. The Trojans are repulsd in the lf!
wing; Hector ftill 15 his ground againſt. the Ajaxes,
"fill being gaul d by the Locrian ſlingers and archers, Po-
Iydamas adwiſes to call a council of war':: Hector af-
proves his advice, but goes firſt to rally the Trojans ; »f-
braids Paris, rejoins Polydamas, meets Ajax again, and
renews the attack.

The eight and twentieth day fj11 continues. The ſcent
is between the Grecian wdif axathe ſea-/hore.
